title: Viral AI & Tech Puzzles (Mind-Blowing Reveals)
licenseNote: Starter questions are bundled for local video generation. Buyers can edit, replace, or extend this JSON file.

items.question: Two AI tools answer a legal question. One invents cases; the other requests sources. Which is safer?
items.answer: The one that asks for sources
items.explanation: A confident answer is not proof. Source-grounded uncertainty is safer than fluent hallucination.
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: A chatbot remembers your first message, then suddenly forgets the document you pasted earlier. Nothing crashed. What invisible limit did you hit?
items.explanation: The model can only keep a limited amount of text in active context.
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: A strong password is reused everywhere. One site is breached, then the cloud account is stolen. What failed?
items.explanation: A strong password still fails if the same one is reused on a breached site.
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: A team runs the same AI prompt twice. One answer is careful, the next is wildly creative. Which setting probably changed?
items.explanation: Temperature controls randomness and variation in model output.
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: Local AI is private but slower; cloud AI is faster but sends data away. What is the tradeoff?
items.explanation: Local compute keeps data under control; cloud compute usually trades privacy for speed.
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: A developer shares a screenshot containing a secret string. Strangers use the paid account. What leaked?
items.explanation: API keys act like software passwords and should never appear in public screenshots.
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: An AI summarizes 200 pages but misses a warning on page 187. What went wrong?
items.explanation: Long documents can exceed what the model actually considers unless the workflow chunks and checks every section.
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: A support email links to a login domain with one changed letter. What attack is this?
items.explanation: The attacker uses a convincing message and lookalike domain to steal credentials.
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: A customer message tells an AI to ignore its rules and reveal them. What attack is this?
items.explanation: Prompt injection hides malicious instructions inside user-controlled text.
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: An AI trained on customer chats later repeats a private phone number. What should have been removed?
items.explanation: Training or retrieval data should be cleaned so private information is not exposed.
items.engagementQuestion: Did this trick you?

items.question: An AI answers confidently but invents the source. What is the failure called?
items.explanation: The model generated plausible text that was not grounded in facts.
items.hint: Look for the ai model clue.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you get it before the reveal?

items.question: What is the hidden unit most chatbots read instead of whole words?
items.explanation: Models split text into token chunks before processing.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you get it before the reveal?

items.question: Why can a GPU train AI faster than a CPU?
items.explanation: GPUs run many similar calculations at the same time.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you get it before the reveal?

items.question: What is a prompt injection trying to change?
items.explanation: It attempts to override or manipulate the system behavior.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you get it before the reveal?

items.question: What protects an account better than a password alone?
items.explanation: A second factor helps even if the password leaks.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you get it before the reveal?

items.question: What makes a website load faster by saving repeated files?
items.explanation: Cached files avoid downloading the same assets again.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you get it before the reveal?

items.question: What is data called after it is labeled for training?
items.explanation: Labels turn raw examples into usable model training material.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you get it before the reveal?

items.question: What tech word means a fake test environment?
items.explanation: A sandbox isolates risky actions from the real system.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you get it before the reveal?

items.question: What does an API key act like?
items.explanation: It authorizes code to use a service.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you get it before the reveal?

items.question: What is the danger of copying secret keys into a public repo?
items.explanation: Anyone can use exposed keys if they find them.
items.engagementQuestion: Did you get it before the reveal?
